lib/postgres-spine.js.reason: mergeEventRows fix for a silent data-loss bug (distinct rows sharing a null/duplicate event_seq collapsed onto the same dedup key). The fix needs a two-key (event_seq, event_id) comparator, which Biome's cognitive-complexity scoring counts as added mass even when extracted into its own named function (compareEventRowOrder) — tried extraction first; it did not reduce the file-level score. Correctness over a lower number here.

lib/spine.ts.reason: loadEventsForSummaries batches the summary-hydration N+1 (one query per correlation id -> one query for the whole page) via a ROW_NUMBER()-partitioned fetch plus a per-id grouping pass (building the id->rows Map, iterating placeholders, hydrating rows). That control flow — not the SQL string literal itself — is what Biome's cognitive-complexity scoring counts as added mass; the SQL text's explicit `(event_seq IS NULL), event_seq, event_id` ordering is necessary because summarizeEvents derives status/source/browser-surface state from array position (first/last event, reverse scans), so the grouped rows must arrive in a caller-guaranteed total order rather than relying on the window function's undocumented partition-membership-only guarantee. This mass was already present at PR #368's head without a justification entry; adding one here rather than reducing it further, since the batching mechanism and its ordering contract are load-bearing correctness, not incidental style.

server/connector-manifest-validation.ts.reason: Port of main's external-tool detection hardening (security fix e6e96933b) + owner-cancel/recovery surfaces from #199/#190 reconciliation; behavior required by main's test contract. Queued for decomposition in the Phase-1 drain.
